title: UDLAP DOCUMENT EXTRACTOR
subtitle: Browser-native AI pipeline that converts bulk institutional scans into structured, exportable data — zero manual entry.
about:
What is the UDLAP Document Extractor?

Institutional environments generate thousands of unstructured physical documents — work orders, supply requests, maintenance logs — that require hours of manual transcription. This React-based utility eliminates that bottleneck by routing raw PDFs and image scans through Vision Language Models, classifying each document by type, extracting its fields into a validated JSON schema, and exporting the aggregated results directly to Excel — all inside the browser, with no backend required.

challenge.title: The Challenge: Unstructured Paperwork at Institutional Scale
challenge.description: Processing high volumes of mixed-format institutional documents manually is error-prone and slow. The system needed to parse bulk PDFs concurrently, survive cloud API rate limits without degrading the UI, and accurately interpret documents with varying physical conditions — faded ink, handwriting, and inconsistent layouts.

cards.title: Chaos to Schema
cards.description: Scanned documents carry no metadata, no consistent structure, and no predictable layout. The system had to infer document type and field boundaries from raw visual context alone.

cards.title: Resilient Async Queue
cards.description: Sequential AI requests with rate limit exposure required a stateful processing queue that could pause, recover, and report real-time ETA — without freezing the browser thread.

solution.title: The Solution: Dual-Provider AI Architecture with Fallback Strategy
solution.description: A custom Service Manager orchestrates document processing through a primary AI provider, automatically falling back to a secondary model on failure — ensuring maximum uptime regardless of individual API availability.

features.title: AI Provider Fallback Pattern
features.description: A dynamic Service Manager switches between Google GenAI (Gemini Flash) and OpenRouter (Qwen-VL) based on live availability, creating a fault-tolerant extraction pipeline.

features.title: Strict JSON Schema Enforcement
features.description: AI responses are validated against typed DTOs at generation time, preventing hallucinated or malformed data from ever reaching the UI layer.

features.title: Decoupled Container Hooks
features.description: All file serialization (PDF-to-Base64) and sequential batching logic lives in isolated Custom Hooks, keeping UI components purely declarative and free of side effects.

features.title: On-Device Archival
features.description: Processed files are automatically renamed using AI-extracted context and bundled into a downloadable ZIP via JSZip — creating a normalized archive without any server involvement.

techStack.title: Technical Depth: The Architecture
techStack.description: A purpose-built frontend pipeline for AI-augmented document intelligence, designed for correctness and browser-native execution.

categories.name: Frontend Engineering
categories.description: React 19 with Vite. Heavy use of useMemo for dashboard performance and strict functional component patterns for predictable, testable UI rendering.

categories.name: Vision Language Models
categories.description: Integrated with the @google/genai SDK and OpenRouter endpoints. JSON Schema mode enforced at the API call level to constrain AI output to well-typed structures.

categories.name: Browser File System
categories.description: Native File API for binary handling, Base64 encoding for multimodal LLM payloads, and JSZip for client-side archive generation — no uploads, no servers.

categories.name: Type Safety
categories.description: End-to-end TypeScript interfaces map arbitrary AI output to strict Data Transfer Objects before any component renders, eliminating runtime type errors.

results.title: Eliminated manual data entry
results.description: for bulk institutional document workflows, reducing processing time from hours to seconds per batch.

results.title: Zero-backend architecture
results.description: The entire pipeline — AI inference, parsing, export, and archival — runs natively in the browser with no server dependency.

results.title: Fault-tolerant by design
results.description: Dual-provider fallback ensures continuous operation even when a primary AI model is rate-limited or unavailable.
